generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates a 60-second automated refresh cycle to ensure real-time situational awareness during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to manually pause the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle and resume it on demand.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 permits manual pause of the refresh cycle to support briefing moments or display freeze requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a Multi-Document Interface (MDI) layout that allocates the left two-thirds of the display area to a graphical timeline view and the right one-third to a data table view. This ratio shall be fixed and not user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 defines fixed display layout proportions to ensure consistent situational awareness across all deployments.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the top one-quarter shall display sequential Events (order-dependent, not time-bound) and the bottom three-quarters shall display Events ordered by temporal proximity to current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 specifies the segmentation of the data table region to distinguish sequential from temporally relevant Events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code all displayed Events and Activities based on their variance from current exercise time, conforming to DHS color schema thresholds for on-time, at-risk, and overdue statuses.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and BR-05 require DHS-compliant color coding to enable instant visual triage of schedule adherence.

generated_requirements.text: All visual elements of the application — including backgrounds, text, highlights, and status indicators — shall conform to the Department of Homeland Security color schema. No custom or ad hoc color choices are permitted without formal DHS approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and RC-01 mandate strict adherence to the DHS color schema for all UI elements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a graphical timeline view in which Events and Activities are plotted as nodes on a timeline graph, showing their scheduled and actual positions relative to current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future state and scope sections require a graphical timeline to provide visual situational awareness of exercise progress.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MSEL Summary data only for each Event and Activity in both the graphical timeline and the data table. Full MSEL detail records shall not be displayed within EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data to keep the display focused and projection-readable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a direct deep-link from each displayed Event or Activity to its corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0), enabling users to navigate to the source record for modification without leaving the display context.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires that all data modifications occur in source applications, and deep-linking supports seamless navigation to those applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall simultaneously display two independent clocks: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time. Both clocks shall be visually distinct and continuously visible on the display.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 mandates dual-clock display to eliminate confusion in exercises where exercise time diverges from local time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define, configure, and modify time segments used to organize and display Events on the timeline. Time segments shall be configurable per exercise and must be set prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires user-configurable time segments to support the organizational structure of each unique exercise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Venue. The filter shall be applicable independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 requires venue-based filtering to reduce cognitive load during fast-moving exercise scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Key Events designation. The filter shall be applicable independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 requires key event category filtering to allow controllers to isolate high-priority events during execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an unfiltered full exercise view that displays all Events and Activities regardless of any active filters, accessible on demand by authorized users.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires an always-accessible unfiltered view to ensure no Events are inadvertently hidden from controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture and log all changes to Event or Activity data — as reflected from source systems — in a persistent history report. This report shall be available for retrieval at any point during or after the exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 mandates a complete audit history to support post-exercise after-action review and reconstruction of the exercise timeline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and make available a complete history report of all Event and Activity data changes at the conclusion of each exercise, suitable for use by After-Action Review analysts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 requires a complete change history report to enable meaningful post-exercise after-action analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate actual versus assumed time variance charts for Events and Activities, providing quantitative schedule variance analysis.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scope section identifies actual versus assumed time variance charts as a required feature for quantitative schedule analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate each user attempting to access EEMDS and shall deny access to any user who is not pre-authorized by the system administrator. No guest or anonymous access modes shall be supported.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 requires positive authentication and denial of unauthorized users to enforce access control policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with an externally managed user identity store (e.g., directory service or administrator-managed list) to resolve authorized user credentials. EEMDS shall not perform user provisioning or identity management.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-04 and the out-of-scope section establish that user identity management is external; EEMDS must consume but not manage it.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 using its existing data interface in read-only mode, consuming MSEL Summary data without modifying any records in the source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and BR-06 require read-only integration with Sync Matrix 1.0 using its existing interface without modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Exercise Management Tool 1.0 using its existing data interface in read-only mode, consuming MSEL Summary data without modifying any records in the source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and BR-06 require read-only integration with EMT 1.0 using its existing interface without modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall consolidate and display Events and Activities from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 in a single unified display without requiring manual data entry by users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 5 requires all Events from both source applications to be surfaced in a single display automatically.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable as a Windows-based client application on a standard PC within the exercise operations center, with no dependency on server-side rendering or cloud infrastructure for core display functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 and the scope section specify a Windows-based MDI client application as the deployment target.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users (Exercise Planners) to configure display settings, including time segment definitions and filter presets, prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scope section includes display configuration and modification capabilities as an in-scope feature for exercise planners.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle and update the display without perceptible degradation in display performance or usability during normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires that the 60-second refresh cycle executes without noticeable impact on display responsiveness.

generated_requirements.text: The data displayed in EEMDS shall never be more than 60 seconds stale relative to the source systems under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 1 sets a maximum data staleness of 60 seconds to ensure real-time situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99% of the time during normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) over the first six months of operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 3 and OC-01 mandate 99% availability during business hours to support reliable exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: All planned maintenance activities shall be scheduled outside of normal business hours (outside 0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) to avoid impacting exercise operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 requires maintenance windows to be outside business hours to protect the 99% availability target.

generated_requirements.text: The display layout, font sizes, and color-coded status indicators shall be legible and interpretable by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of up to 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 and TC-04 require projection-optimized readability at 100 feet for shared situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: Event and Activity detail data displayed in the data table shall be readable by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 30 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 8 sets a 30-foot readability standard for detailed data to support operations center use.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 sets a measurable learnability target to minimize the learning curve for exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall correctly authenticate and grant or deny access to users in 100% of access attempts, with zero unauthorized access incidents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 requires perfect access control enforcement to protect exercise data from unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with all applicable DHS IT security policies governing user authentication and access control for internal operational tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates compliance with DHS IT security policies to meet regulatory and organizational standards.

generated_requirements.text: The history report of Event and Activity data changes shall be tamper-evident, ensuring that logged entries cannot be modified or deleted after they are recorded.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and Business Objective 6 require a reliable audit trail; tamper-evidence ensures the integrity of after-action review data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the full MDI display — including the graphical timeline and data table — correctly on projection equipment capable of displaying a 10×10 screen image, with all layout proportions and font sizes preserved.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 and OC-03 require correct rendering on projection hardware as a primary deployment context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on a standard client PC without requiring modifications to Sync Matrix 1.0 or Exercise Management Tool 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 prohibits modifications to source applications, requiring EEMDS to adapt to existing interfaces.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from a temporary loss of connectivity to either source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0) without crashing, displaying the last successfully retrieved data and indicating the connectivity issue to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 and D-02 identify source application availability as a dependency; graceful degradation prevents total display failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall resume normal data refresh operations automatically once connectivity to a source application is restored, without requiring user intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic recovery from connectivity interruptions supports the 99% availability target and reduces operator burden.

generated_requirements.text: The system's integration layer shall be designed to accommodate future changes to source application data interfaces with minimal rework, isolating integration logic from display logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 and D-02 highlight source application interface stability as a risk; modular integration design reduces maintenance cost.

generated_requirements.text: The system's UI color schema configuration shall be externalized from application code so that updates to the DHS color schema can be applied without requiring a full application rebuild.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and D-03 indicate the DHS color schema may evolve; externalizing it reduces the cost of compliance updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a visible, clearly labeled indicator showing the timestamp of the most recent successful data refresh from each source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users need to know data currency at a glance to assess situational awareness reliability during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall visually indicate to the user when the automatic data refresh is paused, distinguishing the paused state from the active refresh state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users must be aware of the paused state to avoid acting on stale data during briefing or freeze moments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous display of Events and Activities sourced from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 within the same timeline and data table views, with each Event's source application identifiable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consolidating data from two sources in one view requires users to identify the originating system for deep-link navigation.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall record, at minimum, the following for each detected change: the Event or Activity identifier, the field(s) changed, the previous value, the new value, the timestamp of the change, and the source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 requires a complete change history; capturing these fields ensures AAR analysts have sufficient detail for reconstruction.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall be exportable in at least one structured format (e.g., CSV or PDF) to support post-exercise after-action review workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 requires the history report to be available at exercise conclusion; export capability enables offline analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, distinguishing at minimum between the roles of Exercise Controller, Exercise Planner, IT Administrator, and AAR Analyst, with each role granted only the permissions necessary for their function.
generated_requirements.rationale: The stakeholder section defines distinct roles with different access needs; RBAC ensures least-privilege access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly error message when connectivity to a source application is lost, identifying which source is unavailable and the time the connection was lost.
generated_requirements.rationale: Informative error messaging reduces confusion and helps controllers assess the impact of a data source outage during execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to user interactions (e.g., applying a filter, switching views, initiating a deep-link) within 2 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive UI interactions are critical during high-tempo exercise execution to avoid introducing decision-making delays.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all user authentication attempts — both successful and failed — including the username, timestamp, and outcome, in a secure access log accessible only to the IT Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 and Business Objective 7 require auditable access control; logging authentication attempts supports security monitoring.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase and configuration shall be documented sufficiently to allow a qualified system administrator to install, configure, and troubleshoot the application without involvement from the original development team.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 and the IT Administrator stakeholder role require the system to be independently operable and maintainable in the field.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be readable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users. The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory. : 90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events or Activities.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The display shall have two regions: left 2/3 of the display is graphical right 1/3 of the display is a data table
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow modification of the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS property listings by user-specified criteria (e.g., location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms).
generated_requirements.rationale: Core field productivity feature enabling realtors to query MLS data without returning to the office.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS search results including clear property images and relevant listing details.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents need visual property information to evaluate and present listings to clients on-site.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to create new property listings in the MLS from their mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables agents to submit new listings directly from the field without requiring office access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to update existing MLS property listings from their mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents must be able to keep listing data current while working in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all property listing data against defined MLS data validation rules before submitting a new or updated listing to the MLS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-02 to prevent malformed or incorrect data from being introduced into MLS records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA generation is a primary field productivity tool enabling on-site pricing advice to clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly entered property criteria.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-08 to ensure CMA reports are always based on intentional, user-defined inputs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing property locations for a realtor-specified geographic area.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated mapping eliminates the need for separate consumer tools and streamlines the agent's workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide driving directions to a selected property, available only for properties that exist within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-09 and eliminates navigation overhead by integrating directions within the application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to download, view, and manage client contacts synchronized from the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates data fragmentation by giving field agents access to up-to-date client contact information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to download, view, and manage appointments synchronized from the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provides agents with current scheduling information without relying on paper notes or phone calls to the office.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contact and appointment data with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-03, ensuring field data is never more than 60 minutes stale and requires no manual intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor of any new appointments discovered during each automatic synchronization cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-06 to keep agents informed of scheduling changes without requiring manual checks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate calls to sellers or buyers to schedule appointments on behalf of the realtor, using only contact phone numbers associated with that realtor's client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-10 and automates appointment coordination to reduce scheduling lag and manual follow-up.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request initiated by the system, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-06 to eliminate scheduling delays and improve responsiveness to client actions.

generated_requirements.text: All appointment-scheduling communications initiated by the system must be traceable to the realtor's account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-10 accountability requirements and supports compliance with telecommunications regulations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in a read-only offline mode when internet connectivity is unavailable, allowing realtors to access previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-04 to ensure agents remain productive in areas with poor or no connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue write operations that cannot be completed while offline and automatically submit them to the appropriate backend system upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-04 to prevent data loss and ensure field-entered data is eventually persisted to the office system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results for later review, and saved results must remain accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-07 so agents can reference previously retrieved listings without requiring a live connection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save user preferences (including search filters, display settings, and saved results) and restore them fully following any session end, system failure, or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-05 to ensure 100% of saved preferences are restored and agents do not lose personalized configurations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate and be authorized as a registered realtor before accessing any system function.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-01 to ensure only registered realtors can access proprietary MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to any individual who is not formally registered in the organization's realtor registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-01 and industry MLS access regulations requiring authorized membership for data access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support self-service installation on a smart device such that a realtor can complete installation without printed instructions or IT assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates onboarding overhead and enables rapid deployment to a growing agent population.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall run natively on devices operating Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraint specifies these two platforms as the exclusive targets for this release.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target smart device platform, including standard menus and navigation buttons.
generated_requirements.rationale: Platform-native UI conventions reduce the learning curve and ensure the application feels familiar to device users.

generated_requirements.text: MLS property search results shall be returned and displayed to the realtor within 30 seconds of query submission, 100% of the time under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A hard 30-second ceiling is defined as a business success criterion to enable real-time field responsiveness.

generated_requirements.text: CMA reports shall be generated and delivered to the realtor within 60 seconds of criteria entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: A hard 60-second ceiling is defined as a business success criterion to enable on-site pricing advice without delay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, achieving a minimum availability rate of 99.9% (no more than 1 failure per 1,000 access attempts).
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors operate outside standard business hours and require continuous system access to serve clients at any time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated users at launch without degradation of performance or availability.
generated_requirements.rationale: The current registered realtor population is 1,000 and all may be active concurrently in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be scalable to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The organization projects a fivefold growth in registered realtors within 12 months, requiring planned scalability.

generated_requirements.text: At least 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to successfully install the application on their device without printed instructions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Self-service installation is a defined business success criterion to eliminate onboarding overhead at scale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be usable by realtors with no formal training, using terminology and workflows natural to the real estate profession.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminating training requirements is a key business objective to enable rapid onboarding of new agents.

generated_requirements.text: 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored to their last known state following any system failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preference persistence is a defined business success criterion ensuring agents do not lose personalized configurations after failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authentication and authorization on every access attempt, with zero unauthorized access incidents permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: 100% authenticated access is a defined business success criterion protecting proprietary MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the mobile application and backend systems (office system, MLS, authentication server) shall be encrypted in transit using industry-standard protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting sensitive MLS and client data during transmission is essential given the mobile, field-based operating environment.

generated_requirements.text: User credentials and authentication tokens stored on the device shall be protected using platform-appropriate secure storage mechanisms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mobile devices are at higher risk of loss or theft, requiring secure local storage of authentication artifacts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall cache contact, appointment, search result, and CMA data locally on the device to support offline mode operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Local caching is the technical mechanism required to fulfill the offline mode capability defined in BR-04.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the loss of internet connectivity and automatically transition to offline (read-only) mode without requiring realtor intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless offline transition ensures agents experience no disruption when moving into areas with poor connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the restoration of internet connectivity and automatically resume online operations, including submission of any queued write operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic reconnection handling ensures queued data is submitted and the agent returns to full functionality without manual steps.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with a third-party mapping data provider to supply property location maps and driving directions within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Third-party mapping integration is the defined mechanism for delivering navigation capability without building a proprietary mapping engine.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use the smart device's native telephony capability to initiate calls to sellers or buyers for appointment scheduling.
generated_requirements.rationale: Leveraging native telephony fulfills the appointment communication requirement without requiring a separate communication subsystem.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover gracefully from synchronization failures (e.g., office system unavailability) and resume synchronization at the next scheduled interval without data corruption or loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Synchronization failures are an expected dependency risk (D-01) and the system must handle them without compromising data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication attempts, including failures, and all MLS listing creation and update operations, with records traceable to the authenticated realtor's account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logging supports security monitoring, regulatory compliance, and traceability of MLS data changes per BR-10.

generated_requirements.text: The application's locally cached data store shall be sized to accommodate sufficient search results, CMA reports, contacts, and appointments to support a full working day of offline operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adequate local storage capacity ensures offline mode is practically useful for agents working in low-connectivity areas throughout the day.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide clear, in-application feedback to the realtor indicating whether the application is operating in online or offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent connectivity status prevents agents from being unaware that they are viewing potentially stale cached data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display an appropriate error or informational message when a requested operation (e.g., MLS search, CMA generation) cannot be completed due to offline mode or external service unavailability.
generated_requirements.rationale: Informative error messaging prevents agent confusion and supports self-service use without training or IT support.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase and architecture shall be structured to allow independent scaling of backend components (authentication, synchronization, MLS integration) to meet the 5,000-user growth target.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular backend architecture enables IT to scale individual components without a full system redesign as user volume grows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce data validation on all MLS listing fields (e.g., required fields, data type checks, value range checks) and present clear validation error messages to the realtor before submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Client-side validation enforces BR-02 data integrity rules and provides immediate feedback to prevent submission of malformed listings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that appointment-scheduling calls are only placed to phone numbers associated with contacts in the authenticated realtor's own client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-10 to prevent unauthorized or misdirected communications and protect client data privacy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with applicable telecommunications and consumer contact regulations when initiating calls to sellers or buyers for appointment scheduling.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory compliance for consumer communications is explicitly identified as a constraint in section 7.4 of the BRD.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The look and feel of the system shall conform to the user interface standards of the smart device.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user interface shall have standard menus buttons for navigation
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a professional appearance
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall have a consistent color scheme and fonts.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be used by realtors with no training.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be intuitive and self explanatory.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be easy for a realtor to learn.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall use symbols and words that are naturally understandable by the realtor community.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall produce search results in an acceptable time
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The search results shall be returned no later 30 seconds after the user has entered the search criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall generate a CMA report in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The CMA report shall be returned no later 60 seconds after the user has entered the CMA report criteria.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize contacts and appointments in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize with the office system every hour.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available for use 24 hours per day 365 days per year
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Out of 1000 accesses to the system the system is available 999 times.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall operate in offline mode whenever internet connection is unavailable.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to view previously downloaded search results CMA reports and appointments.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall retain user preferences in the event of a failure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored when system comes back online.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able to support multiple remote users
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able support 1000 simultaneous users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be capable of handling the existing 1000 users. This number is expected to grow 5 times within the next year.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be installed by an untrained realtor without recourse to separately-printed instructions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to install the product on their device without printed instructions
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product is expected to run on Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only registered realtors shall be able to access the system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Every user of the system shall be authenticated and authorized.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall prevent its data from incorrect data being introduced.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a real estate agent to query MLS information
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to get driving directions to a selected property
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to download appointments and contact information for clients
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display clear property images in the search results
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall update or create new property listings in the MLS
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Realtor shall be able to generate a CMA report based on property criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be able to call the seller or buyer to schedule an appointment
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be notified of new client appointments after automatic synchronization with office system
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be able to retrieve a map showing property locations for a specified area
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to save the property search results.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR
